description_long: This object is a detailed representation of a lobster, featuring a robust, segmented body with a pronounced cephalothorax and elongated abdomen. The front is dominated by two large, asymmetrical claws (chelae), one typically larger than the other, used for grasping and defense. Along each side of the body, multiple jointed walking legs are visible, with the first pair modified into the prominent claws. The head region displays two long, slender antennae extending forward, and small, rounded eyes set on short stalks. The tail is fan-shaped, composed of several overlapping segments, and the entire surface appears hard and slightly textured, mimicking the exoskeleton of a real lobster. The coloration is a uniform, pale white, lacking any natural pigmentation.
description: A pale, highly detailed lobster with large claws, segmented body, multiple legs, and long antennae. The surface is hard and slightly textured, resembling a real lobster's exoskeleton.
description_view_0: Front view showing both large claws, the head, and antennae extending forward.
description_view_1: Left side view highlighting the body segmentation, walking legs, and the profile of the claws and antennae.
description_view_2: Top-down view displaying the full body segmentation, both claws, and the symmetrical arrangement of legs.
description_view_3: Right side view similar to the left, showing the body profile, legs, and claws from the opposite side.
